regimeSub: Risk-on melt-up meets sticky 4.2% inflation and a hawkish Warsh Fed flirting with a September hike, even as Iran de-escalates.
themes: US-Iran ceasefire caps oil near $73 -> tail risk drains, equities rip to record highs
CPI stuck at 4.2% 3yr high -> Warsh drops cutting bias, September HIKE now live
AI/energy capex boom -> tech leadership powers Dow >52k and SPX 7440 melt-up
Gold pinned near $4000 + DXY soft at 101 -> markets hedge fiscal/Fed-independence risk (Cook ruling)

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y at 4.38 barely above 4.2% CPI; front-end real rate negative — policy not truly restrictive.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bps and steepening; bear-steepener as hike risk meets sticky inflation, not growth optimism.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; risk-on melt-up and Iran de-escalation keep credit complacent despite hike threat.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.6 — calm post-ceasefire, but underprices a live September hike and frayed truce.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73 after US-Iran deal; oil shock unwound but energy still the marginal CPI driver.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: ~$4000 — bid on Fed-independence fears (Cook/Warsh) and persistent inflation, not fear.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1546 weak KRW; soft DXY plus KOSPI 8650 tech mania — chase semis, watch import inflation.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI capex supercycle leading the tape; crowded long, primary engine of the equity melt-up.

headline: Iran ceasefire and a $7trn AI boom drive global equities to records, but 4.2% inflation and a hawkish Warsh Fed have markets pricing the next move as a hike, not a cut.
